statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function that resolves CSS style inheritance for elements in a given HTML document. The function should take an HTML string as input and return the inherited styles for each element. The function should handle the following style attributes: `color`, `background-color`, `font-size`, and `font-family`. Note that the `:root` selector is not supported in this problem. You can assume that the HTML document is well-formed and does not contain any script tags or other embedded code.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the HTML document
statement.outputFormat: A dictionary where the keys are the element IDs and the values are dictionaries representing the inherited styles
statement.notes: You can use the `css` library to parse the CSS styles. The function should be case-insensitive and ignore any styles that are not explicitly defined. You can assume that the HTML document contains only one element with a given ID.

constraints.description: Maximum number of elements in the HTML document

examples.input: Hello
examples.explanation: The function should return the inherited styles for each element.

hints.text: Use the `css` library to parse the CSS styles.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The function should use a recursive approach to resolve the style inheritance. It should start from the root element and traverse the DOM tree to find the inherited styles for each element.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to parse the entire HTML document and then find the inherited styles for each element individually.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a recursive function to traverse the DOM tree and find the inherited styles.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a recursive function to traverse the DOM tree and find the inherited styles. This approach has a time complexity of O(n) where n is the number of elements in the HTML document.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return the correct inherited styles for each element.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where an element does not have a parent element
Not handling the case where an element has multiple parent elements

testCases.input: Hello
Hello
